updated: 2026-07-12 (global_constraints key + graph-mode collision note, from tutorial-notes integration; claims source-verified against wasp core aggregation.py)
provenance: Explainer texts for the v0.4 canvas-organization conventions (PROTOCOL.md 'Generation conventions'). Written for an architecture student reading the canvas cold: 1-3 lines, WHAT the stage does + WHY it exists. Wasp claims cross-checked against knowledge/wasp_patterns.json (mined from the official example corpus, corpus/wasp-upstream) and docs/cookbook.md; per-key evidence listed in 'evidence'.

explainers.part_definition: Turns a Rhino mesh into a named Wasp part: the unit geometry plus numbered connection planes (0, 1, 2, ...) where other parts are allowed to attach. Every module the aggregation places is a copy of this part.
explainers.rules: Rules list the permitted pairings between connections: P|1_P|0 means connection 1 of an existing part P hosts connection 0 of a new P. Rules are directional - write both directions if you want both. More rules = more legal moves = richer, less predictable growth.
explainers.aggregation_stochastic: The growth engine: starting from one part, each step picks a random rule-legal, collision-free placement and attaches a new part, until N parts are placed or no legal moves remain. Same parts + rules + seed reproduce the same structure.
explainers.aggregation_field: The growth engine, steered by a field: candidate placements are ranked by the field value at their location, so the structure grows toward where the field is strong. N caps the part count; colliding placements are always rejected. With several NAMED fields, each part follows the field named in its FIELD input (Advanced Part); unnamed parts default to the FIRST field.
explainers.aggregation_graph: The growth engine, driven by a graph grammar: rules like P|0_P|0>a_b both mate two connections and rewrite graph-node labels, so growth follows the programmed sequence instead of random choice. No part count or seed - the grammar decides what gets built. NOTE: collision checking is OFF in this mode - the grammar author is responsible for avoiding overlaps.
explainers.global_constraints: Global constraints crop growth to a buildable zone: a Plane Constraint keeps parts on one side of a plane, a Mesh Constraint keeps them inside (or outside) a closed volume. They only take effect with the MODE slider at 2 (or 3, which adds local constraints). SOFT=true tests just each part's center (fast); false tests the whole mesh (precise, slower). If nothing places, the seed part sits outside the allowed zone - move it with Transform Part.
explainers.inputs: User-tweakable drivers for the aggregation - change these, not the wiring. Drag the sliders to explore variants; flip reset to True and back to False to force a clean re-grow after editing parts or rules.
explainers.output_geometry: Reads the built result out of the solver: Get Part Geometry pulls each placed part's mesh from the aggregation's PART_OUT output, ready to preview or bake into Rhino. (PART_OUT carries the parts; the AGGR output is the aggregation object itself.)
explainers.output_transforms: Reads the built result as transforms: Deconstruct Part pulls each placed part's 4x4 placement matrix from PART_OUT - lighter than meshes when you want to instance the base geometry yourself.
